items.title: CVE-2026-35616: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Pre-Auth RCE Actively Exploited (CVSS 9.8)
items.summary: A critical improper access control flaw in Fortinet FortiClient EMS versions 7.4.5–7.4.6 allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted API requests. CISA added it to the KEV catalog on April 6, 2026, with a 3-day remediation deadline for federal agencies. Exploitation in the wild was first logged by watchTowr honeypots on March 31, 2026.
items.why: Pre-auth RCE in widely-deployed enterprise endpoint management software with confirmed in-the-wild exploitation before a patch existed. Emergency hotfix is available but full fix targets version 7.4.7.

items.title: CVE-2026-5281: Chrome Zero-Day Use-After-Free in WebGPU (Dawn) Under Active Exploitation
items.summary: A high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Dawn, Chrome's cross-platform WebGPU implementation, was patched and simultaneously added to CISA's KEV catalog on April 1, 2026. The flaw allows remote code execution via a malicious web page, requiring no user interaction beyond visiting a site.
items.why: Zero-day in the world's most-used browser exploitable via any webpage visit. Immediate CISA KEV listing confirms in-the-wild exploitation. All Chrome users on unpatched versions remain exposed.

items.title: Iran-Linked APT 'Screening Serpens' Deploys 6 New RAT Variants Targeting US, Israel, and UAE
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 is tracking Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens,' which sharply escalated espionage operations following the February 2026 regional conflict. Between February and April 2026, the group deployed six previously-undocumented remote access Trojan variants against entities in the US, Israel, UAE, and two other Middle Eastern countries.
items.why: Nation-state campaign using novel, rapidly-diversified malware families across five countries tied to an active geopolitical flashpoint. High malware variant turnover makes IOC-based blocking largely ineffective.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours, Routes C2 Through Cloud Services
items.summary: Trellix researchers uncovered a multi-stage APT28 (Russian GRU) espionage campaign targeting European military and government organizations. The group weaponized the newly disclosed Microsoft Office OLE bypass (CVE-2026-21509) within 24 hours of public disclosure, and tunneled all C2 traffic through legitimate cloud platforms to evade network detection.
items.why: Illustrates the 'negative exploit window' phenomenon—state actors operationalize 1-days before most defenders finish reading the advisory. Cloud-based C2 blends into enterprise traffic and defeats traditional network IOC detection.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Mean Time-to-Exploit Hits -7 Days; Attacker Handoff Collapses to 22 Seconds
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report, drawn from 500,000+ hours of incident response, reveals the mean time-to-exploit has reached -7 days—meaning exploitation now routinely precedes patch availability. Internal attacker handoff time dropped from 8+ hours (2022) to just 22 seconds (2025). Voice phishing surged to 11% of intrusions (overtaking email phishing at 6%), and the BRICKSTORM implant achieved ~400-day dwell times by surviving standard network remediation.
items.why: A -7 day exploit window structurally invalidates patch-as-primary-defense strategies. The 22-second handoff means detection at initial access is the only reliable intervention point before lateral movement is underway.
